title: Day Hospice Care
text:
Day Hospice Care - SupportGoWhere
Day Hospice Care
Share link
A safe and supportive environment for patients with life-limiting illnesses and their caregivers.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Fees payable
Referral required
Collapse all sections
About this support

Services seek to provide holistic care aimed at improving the quality of life, include (but not limited to):

Physical well-being, psychosocial support

Multidisciplinary team of doctors, nurses, medical social workers, art therapist, and spiritual counsellor etc.

Accessible with provision of transportation of patients to and from their homes

Engaging games and activities

Who is this for?

Patient with life-limiting illness (as diagnosed by a doctor):

Who do not require 24-hour nursing assistance, regardless of age, religion, ethnicity, nationality and financial status

With a prognosis of less than 1 year

What to expect?

To qualify for day hospice service, patients have to be referred by their doctors, who should complete a referral form.

Applicants for care services will need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries (if any), and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

How to apply?

You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs.

For further assistance, contact the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk in to a nearby AIC Link.

You can visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

Information last updated on 25 Feb 2026
